info.title: Sarif Pattern Matcher Cli
info.description:
© Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
analyze
analyze-database             Analyze a database.
export-config                Export rule options to an XML or JSON file that
can be edited and used to configure subsequent
analysis.
export-rules                 Export rules metadata to a markdown (*.md) file.
export-search-definitions    Export search definitions file from specific
formats.
import-analyze               Import from and analyze database contents.
export-events                Export ETW events manifest file.
dump-events                  Export ETW events manifest file.
validate                     Validate credentials persisted to SARIF log file.
help                         Display more information on a specific command.
version                      Display version information.

options.name: --connection
options.description:
Required. Connection string to data
source (Kusto).

options.name: --data-type
options.description:
Required. Connection type for the
data. Valid inputs: Kusto, SqlLite.

options.name: --target
options.description:
Required. Data target to analyze
against. For example a Kusto query, or
SqlLite query.

options.name: --batch-size
options.description:
(Default: 0) Number of rows of data to
process per thread.

options.name: --identity
options.description:
Required. Column name from your target
data query/table that can ordered by
for batch processing.

options.name: --vcp
options.description:
A serialized JSON version control
details object to persist to the
output file.

options.name: --dynamic-validation
options.description:
Enable dynamic validations, if any
(may compromise performance and/or
result in calls across the network).

options.name: --disable-dynamic-validation-caching
options.description:
Disable caching from dynamic
validation.

options.name: --enhanced-reporting
options.description:
Enable enhanced reporting provided by
dynamic validators (when

options.name: --retry
options.description: Enable retry connection if enabled.

options.name: --max-memory-in-kb
options.description:
(Default: -1) The maximum memory (in
kilobytes) that can be used for RE2.

options.name: --redact-secrets
options.description:
Redact sensitive credential components
from log files.

options.name: --engine
options.description:
The pattern matching engine to use for
analysis. One of RE2, DotNet, or
CachedDotNet.

options.name: --output
options.description:
File path to which analysis output
will be written.

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
Recurse into subdirectories when
evaluating file specifier arguments.

options.name: --config
options.description:
Path to policy file that will be used
to configure analysis. This defaults
to 'default.configuration.xml' beside
the main tool; passing value of
'default' or removing that file will
configure the tool to use its built-in
settings.

options.name: --quiet
options.description:
Suppress all console output (except
for catastrophic tool runtime or
configuration errors).

options.name: --environment
options.description:
Log machine environment details of run
to output file. WARNING: This option
records potentially sensitive
information (such as all environment
variable values) to any emitted log.

options.name: --plugin
options.description:
Plugin paths, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that points to
plugin(s) that should drive analysis
for all configured scan targets.

options.name: --invocation-properties
options.description:
Properties of the Invocation object to
log, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes. NOTE: StartTime and
EndTime are always logged.

options.name: --rich-return-code
options.description:
Emit a 'rich' return code consisting
of a bitfield of conditions (as
opposed to 0 or 1 indicating success
or failure.

options.name: --trace
options.description:
Execution traces, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that should be emitted
to the console and log file (if
appropriate). Valid values: ScanTime.

options.name: --level
options.description:
Failure levels, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that is used to filter
the scan results. Valid values: Error,
Warning and Note.

options.name: --kind
options.description:
Result kinds, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that is used to filter
the scan results. Valid values: Fail
(for literal scan results), Pass,
Review, Open, NotApplicable and
Informational.

options.name: --baseline
options.description: A SARIF file to be used as baseline.

options.name: --etw
options.description:
A file path to which all ETW events
for the session will be saved.

options.name: --post-uri
options.description:
A URI to which the SARIF log file will
be posted.

options.name: --max-file-size-in-kb
options.description:
The maximum file size (in kilobytes)
that will be analyzed.

options.name: --deny-regex
options.description:
A regular expression used to suppress
scanning for any file or directory
path that matches the regex.

options.name: --log
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as
a semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, for governing output
files. Valid values include
ForceOverwrite, Inline, PrettyPrint,
Minify or Optimize.

options.name: --insert
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as
a semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that should be inserted
into the log file. Valid values
include Hashes, TextFiles,
BinaryFiles, EnvironmentVariables,
RegionSnippets, ContextRegionSnippets,
ContextRegionSnippetPartialFingerprint
s, Guids, VersionControlDetails, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --remove
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as
a semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that should be not be
persisted to or which should be
removed from the log file. Valid
values include Hashes, TextFiles,
BinaryFiles, EnvironmentVariables,
RegionSnippets, ContextRegionSnippets,
Guids, VersionControlDetails, and
NondeterministicProperties.

options.name: --uriBaseIds
options.description:
Key + value pairs, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in
double quotes, that defines a
uriBaseId and its corresponding local
file path. E.g.,
SRC=c:\src;TEST=c:\test

options.name: --sarif-output-version
options.description:
(Default: Current) The SARIF version
of the output log file. Valid values
are OneZeroZero and Current

options.name: --threads
options.description:
A count of threads that should be used
for multithreaded operations.

options.name: --insert-property
options.description:
JSON path + property values, expressed
as a semicolon-delimited list enclosed
in double quotes, that should be
inserted into the output log.
Currently, only paths that point to a
version control provenance property
bag is supported, e.g.,
'runs[0].invocations[1].versionControl
Provenance.properties.myProperty=myVal
ue'.

options.name: --automation-id
options.description:
An id that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Id' property.
See section '3.17.3' of the SARIF
specification for more information.

options.name: --automation-guid
options.description:
A guid that will be persisted to the
'Run.AutomationDetails.Guid' property.
See section '3.17.4' of the SARIF
specification for more information.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
value pos. 0                            One or more specifiers to a file,
directory, or filter pattern that
resolves to one or more binaries to
analyze.

commands.name: export-config
commands.description:
Export rule options to an XML or JSON file that
can be edited and used to configure subsequent

options.name: --format
options.description:
(Default: Json) The file format to persist settings to (if the
format cannot be inferred from the file name extension).

options.name: --plugin
options.description:
Plugin paths, expressed as a semicolon-delimited list enclosed
in double quotes, that will be invoked to retrieve rule
options.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
value pos. 0    Required. Output path for exported analysis options

commands.name: export-rules
commands.description: Export rules metadata to a markdown (*.md) file.

options.name: --search-definitions
options.description:
A path to a file containing one or more search
definitions to drive analysis.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
value pos. 0                Required. Output path for exported rules data as
markdown (*.md).

commands.name: export-search-definitions
commands.description: Export search definitions file from specific

options.name: --input
options.description:
Required. A path to a file that you want to export as search
definition.

options.name: --type
options.description: Required. Type of the input file (BannedApi or Sal).

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description:
Display version information.
value pos. 0    Required. Output path for exported search definitions json.

commands.name: import-analyze
commands.description: Import from and analyze database contents.

options.name: --host
options.description:
Required. The database host from which
we will retrieve data to scan. Create
AppClientId, AppSecret, and
AuthorityId as environment variables.

options.name: --database
options.description:
Required. The database from which we
will retrieve content to scan.

options.name: --query
options.description:
Required. The query that will be used
to retrieve content to scan.

options.name: --retain-downloaded-content
options.description:
If set to true, the downloaded files
won't be deleted.

options.name: --vcp
options.description:
A serialized JSON version control
details object to persist to the
output file.

options.name: --dynamic-validation
options.description:
Enable dynamic validations, if any
(may compromise performance and/or
result in calls across the network).

options.name: --disable-dynamic-validation-caching
options.description:
Disable caching from dynamic
validation.

options.name: --enhanced-reporting
options.description:
Enable enhanced reporting provided by
dynamic validators (when

options.description: Enable retry connection if enabled.

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Validate credentials persisted to SARIF log file.

options.name: --search-definitions
options.description:
A path to a file containing one or more search
definitions to drive analysis.

options.name: --recurse
options.description:
(Default: false) Recursively select
subdirectories in paths.

options.name: --output-directory
options.description:
A directory to output the transformed files to.
If absent, each transformed file is written to
the same directory as the corresponding input
file.

options.name: --log
options.description:
Optionally present data, expressed as a
semicolon-delimited list enclosed in double
quotes, for governing output files. Valid values
include ForceOverwrite, Inline, PrettyPrint,
Minify or Optimize.
